visual_features.overall_style: Straightforward, corporate, image-driven layout with a slightly old‑school Google Sites aesthetic. Large industrial hero photo with bold centered title gives a utilitarian, no‑nonsense feel rather than a refined minimalist one.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is primarily created by size, weight and contrast. The site title in the hero is the largest, boldest white text on a dark image, making it the dominant focal point. The location text below is smaller but still centered. The horizontal main navigation in the hero stands out due to its placement and the green active link. Below, the page section heading in dark gray is the largest text in the content area, followed by regular-weight body text. Sidebar navigation on the left is secondary but noticeable via isolation in a light-gray column and bold active item.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Whitespace is moderate: not overly tight, but not generous either. The hero has ample vertical padding around the title. The main content area provides comfortable padding from the edges and between heading and body copy. The left sidebar is relatively narrow with simple text items spaced by small vertical gaps. Overall, spacing follows a practical grid with clear breathing room between the major blocks (hero, sidebar, main content, image grid) while intra‑component spacing (between nav items, between lines) is compact.
unique_elements: Dual navigation (horizontal navigation overlaying the hero image and a persistent vertical sidebar navigation with the same items).
Full-width photographic hero featuring welding/metalwork, reinforcing the metal fabrication theme.
Thick horizontal divider bar between hero and content, using a solid gray strip instead of a subtle line.
Strong central alignment in the hero contrasted with left-aligned content in the body and sidebar.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12-column-like grid with a full-width hero and a two-column body (narrow sidebar + wider content). Spacing and alignment suggest a standard 8px-based layout typical of Google Sites templates.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Top full-width hero banner with centered content, followed by a two-column body area with left vertical navigation and right main content, all constrained in a central content container.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Sections are stacked vertically: (1) full-width hero with image, title, and horizontal nav; (2) solid gray horizontal separator; (3) content band with left aligned vertical navigation sidebar and main content area; within the main content, sections flow from heading and intro text to a grid of product images/cards.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Hero content (title, location, horizontal nav links) is center-aligned both horizontally and vertically over the background image. Below the hero, the layout switches to a left-aligned, column-based structure: sidebar nav text left aligned, main heading and body text also left aligned. Columns share a top baseline for visual cohesion.
responsive_hints: Hero image and title appear to span full width, suggesting they will scale fluidly with viewport size.
Sidebar plus main content structure hints that on smaller screens the sidebar may collapse above the content as a stacked menu.
Text and spacing look like they are built on a flexible template rather than fixed pixel positions, supporting responsive behavior.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: full-width hero (image + title + top navigation) at the top, followed immediately by a horizontal divider bar. Under the divider, a two-column band: a narrow left sidebar with a vertical list of navigation links, and a wide right content column. Within the right column, a large page title is followed by a paragraph of descriptive copy, then a grid of product photos/cards (not fully visible but clearly arranged in multiple columns). The arrangement supports scanning: hero → section heading → text → examples.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Hero occupies roughly the top 35–40% of the initial viewport height with the photo filling edge to edge. Below, the content band uses the full width but is visually split: approximately 20–25% width for the sidebar navigation and 75–80% for main content. Within the main column, the heading and intro text take the upper portion, while the lower portion is dedicated to product imagery laid out in a grid. Margins around the central content container give a subtle frame but are not overly large.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderate density. The hero feels open due to the large image and large title, whereas the body content is more information-dense: side nav items are relatively close; body text and images have reasonable but not spacious gaps. This results in a functional, slightly compact layout that prioritizes clarity over airy, editorial styling.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Initial eye movement follows an F/Z pattern: starting at the top center with the large site title, moving down to the smaller location text, then to the horizontal nav links. From there, the eye moves downward to the strong gray separator, then to the large main heading in the right content column. After reading the heading and body text, the gaze is drawn downward into the product image grid. The left sidebar nav provides an anchored vertical reference that can capture attention when scanning back up or changing sections.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No distinct, filled CTA buttons are visible. Links are styled as plain text navigation: horizontally in the hero and vertically in the sidebar. The active link appears in a bright green (#00C853) while inactive links are white (on hero) or dark gray/black (in sidebar). Text is medium weight with no visible border or background shape, giving a flat, typographic button appearance.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as simple <a> elements, sometimes inside list items (<ul><li><a>>) for both horizontal and vertical navs. For a primary CTA button, the expected pattern would be an <a> or <button> wrapped in a container for layout, with classes controlling active/hover states.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Product area appears as simple image tiles/cards: rectangular images placed in a grid with consistent aspect ratios. Edges look sharp (0–2px radius). There is little to no shadow; separation is achieved via whitespace and possibly subtle borders. Each card likely includes an image with a small caption or title below in dark gray text. Padding inside the card is minimal, focused on keeping the imagery large.

) using flexbox or CSS grid, with each card as a
containing an followed by a
or

for title/description. Cards are equal-width columns that wrap on smaller screens.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Forms are not visible in this screenshot, but based on the template style, input fields would likely be full-width rectangles with 1px light-gray borders (#BDBDBD), 2–4px corner radius, white background, and simple left-aligned labels placed above each field. Error or focus states would be indicated by a change in border color.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected layout: stacked vertical form with labels above inputs: <form><label>Field</label><input>…</form>. Groups such as contact details may be organized inside <div> wrappers or fieldsets, each with consistent spacing between fields (e.g., 16–24px). Submit likely a text-styled button or minimal filled button using the accent green.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Two navigation presentations: (1) Hero navigation: horizontal row of text links centered over the image, with equal spacing, white text for inactive, and bright green for the active page. No visible underline; emphasis relies on color contrast. (2) Sidebar navigation: vertical list on a light-gray background with each item left-aligned; active item rendered in darker, bolder text, while inactive items are regular weight and dark gray. Overall, the style is flat and typographic with clear state differentiation through weight and color.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented with unordered lists (<nav><ul><li><a>…)) for both horizontal and vertical navs, sharing link targets but different containers for placement. Active state controlled via a class like .active applied to the <li> or <a>. On small screens, these could be merged into a single stacked menu.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary list pattern is the sidebar navigation: low-density vertical list with a line of text per item, no visible separators or bullets, relying on spacing (8–12px vertical gaps) and background color to differentiate. Text is dark enough for clear legibility with a slightly larger size than body text. Inside content, any lists (not visible) would likely be simple bullet lists with standard browser bullets and left indentation.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Implemented via <ul> with <li> elements; bullets are likely removed for the sidebar using CSS (list-style: none), while any content lists would use default or lightly styled <ul>/<ol>. Layout uses block-level flow rather than table-based structures, with flexbox or simple block stacking.

heading.visual_style: Bold, clean sans-serif with relatively uniform stroke widths and a modern but neutral look, giving a straightforward industrial tone.

body.visual_style: Legible, neutral sans-serif for paragraphs, with moderate x-height and good readability at small sizes.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical components (cards, nav items, text blocks) appear to use 16px–24px internal padding horizontally and vertically, aligned to the 8px base unit.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (hero to content, headings to body, sections of imagery) is approximately 32px–48px, providing clear segmentation without feeling overly spacious.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change color on hover (e.g., from white to gray in the hero, from dark gray to green in content) and may show an underline or slight weight increase.
Active state already indicated by bright green for the current page; on click, links probably maintain this color until navigation.
Transitions are expected to be minimal and fast, such as 150–200ms color fades for hover states, aligning with the utilitarian aesthetic.
Hero image could subtly zoom or darken on hover or on load, but the existing design suggests mostly static content with limited animation to keep the experience straightforward.
